entities.name: KRAS

aliases: Kristen Rat Sarcoma viral oncogene homolog

entities.context: Among the most common molecular alterations detected in non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) are mutations in Kristen Rat Sarcoma viral oncogene homolog (KRAS).

entities.name: KRAS G12C
entities.context: The discovery of a small pocket in the binding switch II region of KRASG12C has revolutionized the treatment of KRASG12C-mutated NSCLC patients.

entities.name: KRAS G12C protein
entities.context: The discovery of a small pocket in the binding switch II region of KRASG12C has revolutionized the treatment of KRASG12C-mutated NSCLC patients.

entities.name: Non-Small Cell Lung Carcinoma
entities.context: Among the most common molecular alterations detected in non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) are mutations in Kristen Rat Sarcoma viral oncogene homolog (KRAS).

entities.name: KRAS G12C-mutated Non-Small Cell Lung Carcinoma
entities.context: Sotorasib and adagrasib, direct KRASG12C inhibitors, have been approved by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and other regulatory agencies for patients with previously treated KRASG12C-mutated NSCLC

entities.name: sotorasib
entities.context: Sotorasib and adagrasib, direct KRASG12C inhibitors, have been approved by the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and other regulatory agencies for patients with previously treated KRASG12C-mutated NSCLC

entities.name: immune checkpoint blockade
entities.context: New KRASG12C inhibitors and several combination therapy strategies, including with immune checkpoint inhibitors, are being studied in clinical trials.

entities.name: immune checkpoint inhibitors

entities.name: acquired resistance to KRAS G12C inhibitors
entities.context: Treatment with KRASG12C inhibitors is not curative and patients develop progressive disease, so understanding associated mechanisms of drug resistance is key.
